name: Fruit & Vegetables
description: Apples, oranges, bananas, and the produce aisle's biggest hits.

questions.q: Which fruit has varieties named Cavendish and Gros Michel?

source.notes: Gros Michel was the dominant variety until Panama disease wiped it out in the 1950s.

questions.q: What's the savory cooking-only banana variety used in Caribbean and Latin American cuisine?
Cherry's Latin name 'Prunus avium' refers to which type of cherry?

source.notes: 'Avium' = 'of the birds.' Sour cherry is Prunus cerasus.

questions.q: Which fruit is high in lycopene, the red pigment that's a powerful antioxidant?

source.notes: Watermelon and pink grapefruit also contain lycopene.

questions.q: Botanically speaking, what is a tomato?

source.notes: Botanically a berry. Legally classified as a vegetable in the US since 1893.

questions.q: What's the orange root vegetable rich in beta-carotene?
What fruit is technically the world's most produced fruit?

source.notes: ~180M tonnes per year globally. Banana is #2.

questions.q: What fruit pear variety is named after Enoch Bartlett?

source.notes: Most common in the US; called 'Williams' in the UK.

questions.q: Which leafy green is sometimes called 'rocket' in the UK?
Which iconic apple variety is named after a Tasmanian gardener?

source.notes: Maria Ann Smith ('Granny' Smith) discovered the variety in Australia c.1868.

questions.q: Which pungent root is the cousin of garlic and used heavily in Asian cooking?

source.notes: Ginger, turmeric, and galangal are all rhizomes from the Zingiberaceae family.

questions.q: What's the world's largest type of citrus fruit?

source.notes: Grapefruit is a hybrid of pomelo + sweet orange.

questions.q: Which spicy chili pepper unit measures heat?
Which fruit's name comes from the Algonquian word 'pasiminan,' meaning 'dry fruit'?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced fabricated Persian 'shahdar' etymology with the actual Powhatan/Algonquian origin 'pasiminan' (dried fruit).

questions.q: What's the most popular variety of orange in the US (no seeds, easy peel)?

source.notes: Recognizable by the small 'navel' formation at the bottom.

questions.q: What's the largest commercially-produced melon variety?
What is the British name for what Americans call zucchini?
What is the British name for what Americans call eggplant?

source.notes: Brinjal is the Indian English term — also commonly used.

questions.q: What dark green vegetable was popularized as a healthy 'superfood' by Popeye?

source.notes: Spinach contains iron, but a 1937 misprint made the iron content appear 10× higher than reality, contributing to the legend.

questions.q: What's the world's most expensive spice (by weight)?

source.notes: Costs $5,000-$10,000/kg. Hand-picked stigmas of Crocus sativus.
